Title: Joint LDPC Code and Spreading Optimization for Multi-User Communications
Reason: 다중 접속 채널 LDPC degree distribution 최적화 — 코드 설계(E) 기법이나 다중 접속 특이성 있어 Phase 3 재검토 필요
Authors: Meilin He, Zheng Xing, Zhaoyang Zhang, Rui Guo, Haiquan Wang
Abstract: Closed-form expressions of the degree distribution of regular low-density parity-check codes and spreading length are presented over a Gaussian multiple access channel. Specifically, given a specific variable node degree  $d_{v}$ , the theoretical expressions of check node degree  $d_{c}$  and spreading length m are derived by fixed point equation array. Then, a reliable region where our multi-user system can decode successfully is obtained by solving uncertainty of the inverse problem. Finally, the optimal parameters  $(d_{c}^{o}, d_{v}^{o},m^{o})$  of LDPC code degree distribution and spreading length in the reliable region are selected to maximize the transmission rate. Numerical results show that our derivation is effective and efficient, and exhibits much lower complexity than EXIT chart.

Title: Decoding-Aided Channel Estimation Based on the GC-LDPC Codes in Time-Varying MIMO Systems
Reason: GC-LDPC protograph 설계(PEG + EXIT) 및 error floor 저감 기법 — 코드 설계(E) 이식 가능
Authors: Hongmei Kang, Lin Zhao, Ming Jiang, Chunming Zhao
Abstract: Massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) has been widely adopted in next-generation wireless systems to enhance spectral efficiency and reliability. However, channel aging poses a significant challenge, leading to the outdated channel state information and degraded system performance. To address this issue, we propose a decoding-aided Kalman filter channel tracking algorithm based on the globally-coupled low-density parity-check (GC-LDPC) codes. The proposed approach leverages locally decoded symbols to optimize channel estimation and employs global decoding to further reduce the error floor. Additionally, protograph-based extrinsic information transfer analysis and the error minimization progressive edge-growth algorithm are utilized to optimize the compatible GC-LDPC codes. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm significantly improves channel tracking performance in time-varying MIMO systems.

Title: Reduced Complexity Interval Passing for Sparse Signal Recovery
Reason: LDPC Tanner graph 기반 sequential BP 스케줄링에서 영감받은 IPA — 디코더 스케줄링(C) 이식 가능성 있으나 CS 복원 주목적이므로 Phase 3 재검토 필요
Authors: Salman Habib, Rémi A. Chou, Taejoon Kim
Abstract: The reconstruction of sparse signals from a limited set of measurements poses a significant challenge as it necessitates a solution to an underdetermined system of linear equations. Compressed sensing (CS) deals with sparse signal reconstruction using techniques such as linear programming (LP) and iterative message passing schemes. The interval passing algorithm (IPA) is an attractive CS approach due to its low complexity when compared to LP. In this paper, we propose a sequential IPA that is inspired by sequential belief propagation decoding of low-density-parity-check (LDPC) codes used for forward error correction in channel coding. In the sequential setting, each check node (CN) in the Tanner graph of an LDPC measurement matrix is scheduled one at a time in every iteration, as opposed to the standard “flooding” interval passing approach in which all CNs are scheduled at once per iteration. The sequential scheme offers a significantly lower message passing complexity compared to flooding IPA on average, and for some measurement matrix and signal sparsity, a complexity reduction of approximately 36% is achieved. We show both analytically and numerically that the reconstruction accuracy of the IPA is not compromised by adopting our sequential scheduling approach.

Title: GNN-Based Auto-Encoder for Short Linear Block Codes: A DRL Approach
Reason: DRL+GNN 기반 패리티검사행렬 설계 및 탠너그래프 메시지패싱 디코더 — 코드설계(E)·신경망 디코더(C) 이식 가능
Authors: Kou Tian, Chentao Yue, Changyang She, Branka Vucetic, Yonghui Li
Abstract: This paper presents a novel auto-encoder based end-to-end channel encoding and decoding. It integrates deep reinforcement learning (DRL) and graph neural networks (GNN) in code design by modeling the generation of code parity-check matrices as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), to optimize key coding performance metrics such as error-rates and code algebraic properties. An edge-weighted GNN (EW-GNN) decoder is proposed, which operates on the Tanner graph with an iterative message-passing structure. Once trained on a single linear block code, the EW-GNN decoder can be directly used to decode other linear block codes of different code lengths and code rates. An iterative joint training of the DRL-based code designer and the EW-GNN decoder is performed to optimize the end-end encoding and decoding process. Simulation results show the proposed auto-encoder significantly surpasses several traditional coding schemes at short block lengths, including low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes with the belief propagation (BP) decoding and the maximum-likelihood decoding (MLD), and BCH with BP decoding, offering superior error-correction capabilities while maintaining low decoding complexity.

Title: A Posteriori Guessing Random Additive Noise for Lossless Source Coding With Side Information
Reason: AP-GRAND는 LDPC 포함 모든 채널 코드에 적용 가능한 범용 디코더(C)로 명시
Authors: Javier Camino Treviño, Meryem Benammar, Damien Roque
Abstract: We propose a maximum a posteriori (MAP)-approaching decoder, namely a posteriori guessing random additive noise decoding (AP-GRAND), which generalizes the existing maximum likelihood (ML)-approaching guessing random additive noise decoding (GRAND) to non-uniform sources. This decoder is notably useful for lossless source coding with side information (LSCSI) problems involving non-uniform binary sources. The proposed decoder is universal and can be applied to all channel codes. We illustrate the performance of AP-GRAND for short-blocklength Polar, BCH, and LDPC codes.

Title: When Can an Expander Code Correct Ω(n) Errors in O(n) Time?
Reason: Expander(Tanner) code O(n) 디코딩 조건·알고리즘 제시 — LDPC는 Tanner code 특수형이므로 디코더 알고리즘(C) 이식 가능
Authors: Yuanting Shen, Chong Shangguan, Minghui Ouyang, Kuan Cheng
Abstract: Tanner codes are error-correcting codes built from a bipartite graph G and a short inner code  $C_{0}$ . Expander codes are a special type of Tanner code, where the graph is highly interconnected, ensuring stronger error correction capabilities. This paper is motivated by the following natural and fundamental problem in decoding expander codes: What are the sufficient and necessary conditions that  $\delta \in [{0,1}]$  and  $d_{0}\in \mathbb {N}$  must satisfy, so that every bipartite expander G with vertex expansion ratio  $\delta $  and every linear inner code  $C_{0}$  with minimum distance  $d_{0}$  together define an expander code that corrects  $\Omega (n)$  errors in  $O(n)$  time? For  $C_{0}$  being the parity-check code, the landmark work of Sipser and Spielman (IEEE-TIT’96) showed that  $\delta \gt 3/4$  is sufficient; later, Viderman (ACM-TOCT’13) improved this to  $\delta \gt 2/3-\Omega (1)$  and he also showed that  $\delta \gt 1/2$  is necessary. For general linear code  $C_{0}$ , the previously best-known result of Dowling and Gao (IEEE-TIT’18) showed that  $d_{0}=\Omega (c\delta ^{-2})$  is sufficient, where c is the left-degree of G. We present a near-optimal solution to the above problem for general  $C_{0}$  by showing that  $\delta d_{0}\gt 3$  is sufficient and  $\delta d_{0}\gt 1$  is necessary, thereby significantly improving Dowling-Gao’s result. To prove the sufficient condition, we present two novel algorithms for decoding arbitrary expander codes with  $\delta d_{0}\gt 3$ , where the first algorithm is deterministic, and the second one is randomized and has a larger decoding radius. To prove the necessary condition, we generalize the aforementioned necessary result of Viderman, and construct for every pair of  $\delta,d_{0}$  with  $\delta d_{0}=1$ , an expander code with constant distance, that only corrects a constant number of errors.

Title: SKI-SAT: A CMOS-Compatible Hardware for Solving SAT Problems
Reason: CMOS Ising 머신으로 LDPC 디코딩 포함 최적화 문제 해결 — 아날로그 HW 아키텍처(D) 이식 가능성 애매, Phase 3 재검토
Authors: Ahmet Yusuf Salim, Bart Selman, Henry Kautz, Zeljko Ignjatovic, Selçuk Köse
Abstract: Nature-inspired computation is receiving increasing attention. Various Ising machine (IM) implementations have recently been proven to be effective in solving numerous combinatorial optimization problems including maximum cut, low density parity check (LDPC) decoding, and Boolean satisfiability (SAT) problems. In this paper, a novel method is presented to solve SAT or MAX-SAT problems with a CMOS circuit implementation. The technique solves a SAT problem by mapping the SAT variables onto quantized capacitor voltages generated by an array of nodes that interact through a network of coupling units. The nodal interaction is achieved through coupling currents produced by the coupling units, which charge or discharge capacitor voltages, implementing a gradient descent along the SAT problem’s cost function to minimize the number of unsatisfied clauses. The system also incorporates a unique low-complexity perturbation scheme to avoid settling in local minima, greatly enhancing the performance of the system. The simulation results demonstrate that the proposed SKI-SAT is a high-performance and low-energy alternative that surpasses existing software-based SAT solvers by significant margins, achieving more than 10 times faster solution and over 300 times less power.

Title: VLSI Architecture for High-Speed Soft-Decision Decoding Using ORBGRAND
Reason: ORBGRAND 알고리즘 기반 파이프라인·병렬화 VLSI 아키텍처(FPGA 합성 포함)로 소프트-결정 디코더 HW 구현 기법(D)이 NAND LDPC 디코더에 이식 가능.
Authors: S. Palanivel Rajan, M. Paramaiyappan
Abstract: Soft-decision decoding plays a crucial role in achieving near-capacity error correction performance for modern communication standards such as 5G and 6G. However, existing soft-decision decoders often suffer from high complexity, large area overhead, and limited throughput, making them less suitable for high-speed applications. This paper proposes a high-throughput VLSI architecture for soft-decision decoding based on the Ordered Reliability Bits Guessing Random Additive Noise Decoding (ORBGRAND) algorithm. The novelty of the proposed approach lies in its pipelined and parallelized noise-guessing engine, lightweight reliability sorting mechanism, and optimized memory hierarchy, which collectively minimize latency and improve energy efficiency. The overall methodology involves algorithmic optimization, architecture design in HDL, simulation under AWGN channels, FPGA synthesis, and benchmarking against existing GRAND-based decoders. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed architecture achieves multi -Gbps throughput, lower decoding latency, and superior error-correction performance with reduced area and power consumption. These results highlight its potential as an efficient solution for next-generation high-speed communication systems.

Title: BER Optimization in Low-SNR Regimes using LDPC Codes
Reason: 저 SNR 환경에서 하드·소프트 결정을 동적 결합하는 Hybrid Bit-Flipping LDPC 디코더 제안 — 이식 가능한 디코더 알고리즘(C항)
Authors: Aarush Agarwal, Anand Mohandas
Abstract: In this study, we address the challenge of optimizing Bit Error Rate (BER) performance in low-SNR scenarios for 6G wireless communication systems. Initially, we establish a confusion area around the received BPSK symbols by defining the decision using the standard deviation of the AWGN noise. This probabilistic thresholding allows more accurate classifications of potentially incorrect bits. We suggest a novel Hybrid Bit-Flipping LDPC decoding technique that dynamically combines both hard and soft decision strategies to improve the decoding process further. Our approach dramatically lowers BER, particularly in very noisy environments, by using the probabilistic confidence of each bit in the confusion region. Lastly, simulation results show that under limited SNR conditions, the suggested hybrid decoder performs better than conventional LDPC decoding schemes in terms of preserving data integrity.

Title: Efficient Design of MET-LDPC Code Ensembles via Multinomial Parameter Optimization
Reason: MET-LDPC 코드 앙상블 설계(엣지 분포·노드 프로파일 최적화, 디코딩 임계값 극대화) — NAND LDPC 코드 설계에 직접 이식 가능한 기법(E).
Authors: Linh Nguyen, Tuy Tan Nguyen
Abstract: Designing multi-edge type low-density parity-check (MET-LDPC) code ensembles that satisfy structural constraints while achieving near-capacity performance remains a challenging problem. Existing methods, including those based on differential evolution, have improved flexibility but still face limitations such as high computational cost and the need for manual configuration of edge patterns. We propose an optimization-based method that explores diverse, structurally feasible edge distribution candidates and optimizes node distributions via a constrained linear system formulation. A dual annealing optimizer is then applied to identify node profiles that maximize the decoding threshold. Evaluations on both the binary erasure channel and the binary-input additive white Gaussian noise channel across multiple code rates show that our method consistently produces ensembles with strong threshold performance and low edge complexity, highlighting its practicality and adaptability.

Title: Adaptive AI ECC Hybrid Burst Error Correction using Deep Learning for Next Generation Communication and Memory Devices
Reason: QLC NAND 플래시 대상 포함, 딥러닝 버스트 검출+LDPC 디코딩 하이브리드 — NAND 직접+이식 가능 디코더(A/C)
Authors: Shiv Prasad Lingampally, Chakradhar Adupa, Gyanendra Kumar Maurya
Abstract: Burst errors—extended runs of consecutive bit corruptions—pose a fundamental challenge for next-generation systems such as 6G wireless networks and QLC NAND flash memories. Conventional error correction codes (ECC), such as LDPC and BCH, offer strong protection against random noise but deteriorate markedly when faced with structured burst patterns. Conversely, AI-based detectors can localize burst-prone regions but lack mathematically grounded correction. To bridge this gap, we introduce a hybrid error-control framework that couples deep learning models (CNNs, LSTMs, and Transformers) for burst-region detection with LDPC decoding to ensure reliable correction. Evaluation was carried out using synthetic datasets together with real-world traces covering wireless, optical, and memory use cases. Experimental evaluations confirm consistent gains in bit error rate (BER), burst coverage ratio (BCR), and decoding latency, outperforming both ECC-only and AI-only baselines. Beyond performance gains, the design emphasizes explainability, scalability, and hardware readiness, thereby positioning it as a strong candidate for real-time deployment in future communication networks and high-density storage systems.

Title: On the Convergence Speed of Spatially Coupled LDPC Ensembles Under Window Decoding
Reason: SC-LDPC window decoding 수렴속도 분석 — iteration 설계에 도움되는 코드설계/디코더 기법(C/E) 이식 가능
Authors: Qingqing Peng, Dongxu Chang, Guanghui Wang, Guiying Yan
Abstract: It is known that windowed decoding (WD) can effectively balance the performance and complexity of spatially coupled low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes. In this study, we show that information can propagate in a wave-like manner at a constant speed under WD. Additionally, we provide an upper bound for the information propagation speed on the binary erasure channel, which can assist in designing the number of iterations required within each window.

Title: On the Derivative Structure of Euclidean Geometry Codes
Reason: 유한기하(EG)-LDPC 부호의 derivative 구조·sum-product 디코딩 — 이식 가능한 코드설계+디코더 기법(E/C)
Authors: Jialong Leng, Chao Chen, Ling Liu, Baoming Bai, Xiaotian Wang
Abstract: Recently, Huang and Zhang [1], [2] introduced the derivative as a fundamental structure of cyclic codes, based on which the derivative decoding was further presented. It is well known that some cyclic codes constructed based on finite geometries form a special class of low-density parity-check (LDPC) codes, which perform well under the sum-product algorithm. In this paper, we study the derivative structure of Euclidean geometry (EG) codes, with a special interest in EG-LDPC codes. It is proved that for µ = m −2, the derivative ascendant of the extended (µ,s)th-order EG code of length 2ms is the extended (µ+1,s)th-order twofold EG code of length 2ms. As a subcode of EG-LDPC code, the derivative descendant of the extend (1,s)th-order twofold EG code is characterized in terms of the roots of the generator polynomial. The new code relationship suggests that the twofold EG code can leverage the sum-product decoding of the derivative descendant for its derivative decoding.

Title: An Improved Decimation Technique for Erasure Decoding of Quantum LDPC Codes
Reason: QLDPC erasure 디코더지만 BP-DD(degree-based decimation) BP 변형은 고전 LDPC 디코더로 이식 가능(C); 애매하여 살림
Authors: Gayathri R., Shobhit Bhatnagar, Abhinav Vaishya, P Vijay Kumar
Abstract: Quantum low density parity-check (QLDPC) codes represent an attractive candidate for achieving fault-tolerant quantum computation. Erasure decoding of QLDPC codes has gained traction recently as many physical systems such as neutral-atom systems, photonic systems, trapped-ion systems etc. suffer from qubit erasures. Techniques for erasure decoding of QLDPC codes via belief propagation (BP) have been proposed in the literature, such as BP with guided decimation (BP-GD), where decimation refers to sequentially fixing hard values for the variable nodes. We provide an alternative decimation-based BP algorithm, which we term the BP with degree-based decimation (BP-DD) algorithm, that intelligently chooses a check node based on its degree and subsequently decimates a variable node in its neighborhood. We apply the BP-DD algorithm to two families of QLDPC codes, namely hypergraph product codes and lifted product codes, and show improved logical error performance over the BP-GD algorithm, without incurring additional complexity. Our simulations show that the BP-DD algorithm provides a versatile solution for erasure decoding of QLDPC codes, in contrast to some techniques in the literature that are applicable to only specific classes of QLDPC codes.

Title: A Decoder with Reinforcement Learning Feedback
Reason: bit-flipping 디코더를 RL 피드백으로 강화하는 이식 가능 디코더 알고리즘(C); BSC 실험
Authors: Milad Taghipour, Bane Vasić
Abstract: This paper explores the application of reinforcement learning techniques to improve the performance of bit-flipping decoders and finding their optimal decisions. We begin by providing an overview of bit-flipping based decoders and reinforcement learning algorithms. We then outline the methodology for mapping the iterative decoding process into Markov Decision Processes (MDPs). We propose a feedback-based method to exploit and enhance the performance of existing well-established decoders by applying reinforcement learning algorithms after a selected decoder. In essence, we modify the MDP to reduce the number of states, making reinforcement learning algorithms feasible for low-rate and long-length codes. In addition, focusing on the correction of dominant error patterns of a selected decoder improves its error correction capability. Finally, we present experimental results for the Binary Symmetric Channel (BSC) to demonstrate the efficiency of the proposed methods.

Title: Early Termination of Low-Density Parity-Check Codes for Continuous-Variable Quantum Key Distribution
Reason: CV-QKD reconciliation의 log a-posteriori 조기종료(early termination) — 디코더 처리량 개선 기법이 NAND LDPC 디코더에 이식 가능(C), 애매하여 살림
Authors: Kadir Gumus, Joao dos Reis Frazao, Vincent van Vliet, Menno van den Hout, Aaron Albores-Mejia, Thomas Bradley, Chigo Okonkwo
Abstract: We analyse the impact of log a-posteriori early termination on the decoding throughput of reconciliation for continuous-variable quantum key distribution. Increases in decoded secret key rate throughput of up to 182% are reported in both simulations and experiments. © 2025 The Author(s)

Title: LDPC Coding for Bursty Optical Channels
Reason: 버스트 채널용 LDPC 디코딩, burst-aware LLR·채널상태추정 — 이식 가능 디코더 알고리즘(C), NAND 버스트성 에러에 적용 가능
Authors: Han Cui, Magnus Karlsson, Erik Agrell
Abstract: We propose an LDPC decoding scheme for bursty residual phase noise channels, using Viterbi-based channel state estimation and burst-aware LLRs. Simulations show improved bit and packet error rates over conventional schemes with and without interleaving. ©2025 The Author(s)

Title: Evaluation of 50G-PON FEC Tolerance to Receiver Impairments
Reason: 50G-PON LDPC(17280,14592) hard/soft-input 소프트디코딩의 BER·디코더 반복수 평가 — 소프트 디코딩/LLR 관점이 NAND 연질판정과 연관, 애매하여 살림
Authors: L. Anet Neto, D. Chevalier, G. Simon, L. Inglés, J. Potet, P. Chanclou, R. Pyndiah
Abstract: We experimentally investigate the impacts of jitter and decision threshold resolution in a 50G-PON downstream scenario supported by an LDPC(17280, 14592) FEC. Both hard and soft input with a soft decoding scheme are evaluated in terms of BER and mean number of decoder iterations. © 2025 The Author(s)

Title: Error Correction Code Acceleration in Future RAN: Challenges and Opportunities
Reason: ECC 가속(ASIC/FPGA) 및 새 인코딩·디코딩 알고리즘, AI-ECC 서베이 — NAND LDPC HW/디코더에 이식 가능한 가속 기법 정리(D/C)
Authors: Minwoo Kim, Hyun Jong Yang
Abstract: With the proposal of new communication enhancement axes such as ultra-reliable low-latency communications (URLLC), researchers are targeting to develop a fast and error-free communication technology in many aspects. In particular, this paper aims to provide a survey on the recent error correction code (ECC) optimization research. We focus on the acceleration of ECC using novel design of application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs), field-programmable gate arrays (FPGAs), and new encoding and decoding algorithms. We also address artificial intelligence (AI)-based ECCs. We discuss their advantages, limitations, and their potential in future network environments.

Title: GPU-Accelerated Syndrome Decoding for Quantum LDPC Codes below the 63 μs Latency Threshold
Reason: QLDPC용 GPU 병렬 BP(syndrome) 디코더 — 병렬화된 belief propagation 디코더 알고리즘·병렬화 기법이 NAND LDPC 디코더로 이식 가능(C, 애매하나 디코더 기법 명확)
Authors: Oscar Ferraz, Bruno Coutinho, Gabriel Falcao, Marco Gomes, Francisco A. Monteiro, Vitor Silva
Abstract: This paper presents a GPU-accelerated decoder for quantum low-density parity-check (QLDPC) codes that achieves sub-63 μs latency, below the surface code decoder’s real-time threshold demonstrated on Google’s Willow quantum processor. While surface codes have demonstrated below-threshold performance, the encoding rates approach zero as code distances increase, posing challenges for scalability. Recently proposed QLDPC codes, such as those by Panteleev and Kalachev, offer constant-rate encoding and asymptotic goodness but introduce higher decoding complexity. To address such limitation, this work presents a parallelized belief propagation decoder leveraging syndrome information on commodity GPU hardware. Parallelism was exploited to maximize performance within the limits of target latency, allowing decoding latencies under 26 μs for [[784, 24, 24]] codes and as low as 16.9 μs for smaller codes, meeting the tight timing constraints of superconducting qubit cycles. These results show that real-time, scalable decoding of asymptotically good quantum codes is achievable using widely available commodity hardware, advancing the feasibility of fault-tolerant quantum computation beyond surface codes.

Title: FPGA Design and Implementation of an LDPC Encoder Under a Hybrid Sparse Matrix Architecture
Reason: 하이브리드 희소행렬 기반 FPGA LDPC 인코더 아키텍처(메모리·병렬성·전력 개선) — 이식 가능 HW 아키텍처(D)
Authors: Yu Chen, Xiaowei Wu, Yali Lv, Xinling Wen, Heng Yang, Yuxin Qin, Teng Jiang, Kun Liang
Abstract: Low-Density Parity-Check (LDPC) codes have been extensively applied in wireless communications and data storage systems. However, their hardware encoder implementations often suffer from excessive memory consumption, limited parallelism, and high power dissipation. To address these issues, this paper presents an FPGA-oriented LDPC encoder architecture based on a hybrid sparse matrix. The proposed method decomposes the generator matrix $G$ into a strictly sparse matrix and a small residual matrix, with the latter approximated through low-rank or sparse small-core techniques. A reconfigurable small-core computing module is introduced to efficiently process the residual part, minimizing redundant computations. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed design reduces LUT and DSP utilization by approximately 20%, increases the operating frequency by 25%, and lowers power consumption by about 20%. Moreover, under the same signal-to-noise ratio, the bit error rate exhibits faster convergence compared to conventional encoder architectures.

Title: Parallelization Optimization and Performance Analysis of LDPC Decoding Based on Reconfigurable Logic Architecture
Reason: 재구성 가능 로직 기반 LDPC 디코딩 병렬화, 비균일 노드 그룹핑·적응 자원스케줄링 — 이식 가능 HW 아키텍처/병렬화(D)
Authors: Yichen Wang, Chenghao Han
Abstract: Low-Density Parity-Check (LDPC) decoding performance is sought to be ameliorated via a parallelization regime that harnesses reconfigurable logic architectures. A non-uniform node grouping mechanism rooted in the density distribution of the parity-check matrix is proposed to boost parallelism while curbing decoding performance degradation, with a dynamically reconfigurable operation unit array constructed to accommodate diverse code lengths and rates and paired with an adaptive resource scheduling strategy that cuts down resource redundancy. Such a combined approach resolves key constraints of traditional decoding architectures, including insufficient parallelism for high-data-rate scenarios, excessive resource redundancy, and poor adaptability to varying code parameters. Extensive empirical tests demonstrate that a decoding throughput of 3.2 Gbps is achieved at a parallelism degree of 16 (a fourfold increase over serial decoding), alongside an 85.2% resource utilization rate and 51% improved energy efficiency, with bit error rate (BER) performance deterioration restricted to within 0.15 dB—thus validating the scheme’s effectiveness for high-speed communication systems.

Title: New binary quantum codes from Hermitian dual-containing Quasi-cyclic codes
Reason: GF(4) 2-generator quasi-cyclic 부호 구성 — 양자코드 도출용이나 기반 고전 QC 부호설계(E)를 독립적으로 떼어낼 수 있음; 애매하여 in(Phase3 재검토)
Authors: Liangdong Lu, Gaochi Zhang, Ganyu Feng, Wenzheng Ma
Abstract: In this paper, we introduce a class of dualcontaining 2 -generator quasi-cyclic codes over $G F(4)$. By Hermitian construction, we show computation constructions of binary quantum codes with good parameters from quaternary quasi-cyclic codes. Furthermore, 3 new quantum codes [[42, 10, 9]], [[74,,36, 9]] and [[78, 40, 9]] are derived from Hermitian dual-containing quasi-cyclic codes by an exhaustive search, along with 24 newly derived codes that enhance the previously best-known lower bounds on minimum distance.

Title: Efficient Shuffled Sliding Window Decoding of SC-LDPC Codes
Reason: SC-LDPC 슬라이딩윈도우 디코딩 변형(λMDQ-SWD)+CN update 90nm HW 구현 — 이식 가능한 디코더 알고리즘·HW(C/D)
Authors: Qin Du, Lisha Luo, Xuan He
Abstract: Spatially coupled low-density parity-check (SCLDPC) codes provide excellent performance and low latency under sliding window decoding (SWD). To achieve a low-power and low-complexity SC-LDPC decoder, we propose a variant of SWD called size-$\lambda$ monotonic double-ended queue SWD ($\lambda$ MDQSWD), where $\lambda$ refers to the storage complexity of the update at a check node (CN). Our $\lambda$ MDQ-SWD employs the shuffled schedule in SWD and uses the MDQ structure to implement the CN update. Moreover, it considers using only the minimum magnitude from target symbols for the CN update to avoid redundant computations. Simulation results show that, although under $\lambda=2$, the proposed algorithm achieves comparable error correction performance to the state-of-the-art algorithms. Moreover, a detailed hardware implementation for the CN update is designed. Analysis using the 90 nm CMOS technology library shows that, compared to the state-of-the-art algorithms, the $\lambda$ MDQ-SWD with $\lambda=2$ reduces the area and increases the clock frequency of CN update by 63.7 % and 107.6 %, respectively.

Title: A Reconfigurable LDPC Decoder for NAND Flash Supporting Codeword Lengths Up to 4kB
Reason: NAND 플래시용 재구성 QC-LDPC 디코더 HW(22nm, 4kB) — NAND 직접+HW 아키텍처(A/D)
Authors: Junyi Wang, Yanling Jiang, Liang Zhang, Humiao Li, Zhixiong Li, Yida Li, Longyang Lin
Abstract: This work presents a reconfigurable quasi-cyclic low-density parity-check (QC-LDPC) decoder supporting NAND flash memories with codeword lengths up to 4 kB. The proposed design provides cross-standard compatibility while enabling dynamic performance scaling to optimize throughput and power for diverse application scenarios. Implemented in 22 nm CMOS, the core occupies 1.79 mm2 and achieves 20.3 Gb/s maximum throughput.

Title: Decoding of LDPC Codes Combined with M-Ary Spread Spectrum Signals for Underwater Acoustic Communications
Reason: SPA 변형인 Soft Distance Decoding(SDD)을 제안 — 스케일링 불필요한 새 디코딩 메트릭. 수중음향 응용이나 디코더 기법(C) 이식 가능, 살림
Authors: Chenwanli Peng, Martin Johnston, Benjamin Sherlock
Abstract: In this paper, we propose a decoding metric for the Sum-Product algorithm (SPA) that allows us to combine LDPC codes with spreading codes for underwater acoustic communications and directly use correlator values at the receiver to iteratively decode and retrieve the original LDPC-encoded messages. This variation of the SPA is known as Soft Distance Decoding (SDD). The conventional SPA requires accurate log-likelihood ratios to perform optimally but it is challenging to obtain this from correlator values. We consider M-ary spreading codes combined with several different CCSDS-standard LDPC codes and compare the BER performance of SDD with conventional SPA and also the Min-Sum algorithm (MSA) on a multipath fading channel. We find that the conventional SPA requires the despreading sequence values to be scaled to achieve optimal performance. However, this scaling varies depending on a number of factors such as the type of channel and LDPC code parameters, and therefore determining the optimal scaling is complicated. The Min-Sum algorithm (MSA) and the SDD do not require initial values to be scaled, but we find that SDD always achieves the optimal performance and outperforms the MSA.

Title: Layered Decoding of DGLDPC Codes Based on a SPC-PC Codes
Reason: DGLDPC에 layered scheduling decoding 적용 — 빠른 수렴의 layered 디코딩 기법(C). 바이너리 SPC 기반, 이식 여지 있어 살림
Authors: Siqi Yu, Zhiping Shi, You Zhang, Wangning Liu
Abstract: In optical communication systems, channel coding is required to exhibit high code rates, superior performance, high throughput, and low complexity. While LDPC codes, adopted as the channel coding standard for 5G, deliver commendable performance, they often struggle to simultaneously achieve high code rates and low bit error rates. In contrast, DGLDPC codes, as an extension of LDPC codes, have been demonstrated to maintain excellent performance even at high code rates. This paper investigates a type of DGLDPC code and integrates it with layered scheduling decoding which is a technique originally developed for LDPC codes. The aim is to leverage their shared advantages: high error-correcting performance, strong structural regularity, and rapid convergence speed.

Title: LNOMS-SS: A Low-Complexity LDPC Decoding Algorithm with Hardware Implementation
Reason: LNOMS-SS 신규 layered min-sum 변형 + 8레벨 디코더 HW 구현, 이식 가능 디코더/HW(C/D)
Authors: KaiShi Wang, Cheng Wang, Songsong Cai, Jiewen Jiang, Xi Gong, Weidong Wang
Abstract: With the development of low orbit satellite communication technology, how to improve the communication speed of satellite communication systems and reduce the complexity of system design has become an important research issue. Since the invention of LDPC codes, their excellent decoding performance has been favored by researchers. QC-LDPC codes have become the channel coding scheme for the fifth generation new radio (5G NR) data channel, but at the same time, it has also brought challenges to hardware design. We propose the Layered Normalized Offset Minsum and three layer static scheduling (LNOMS-SS) algorithm. On the basis of the traditional Layered Normalized Minsum algorithm (LNMS) and Layered Offset Minsum algorithm (LOMS), the update order of each layer during hierarchical decoding is determined based on the row weight, column weight, and the number of perforated non-zero elements in the BG matrix. We also implemented the algorithm in hardware and designed an 8-levels LDPC decoder. We proposed innovative designs such as a pre-processing based routing network, a segment approximation based Minsum calculation module, and an improved checksum module. The simulation and implementation results show that our proposed LNOMS-SS algorithm has stronger performance than other algorithms, with lower hardware resource consumption and decoding latency.

Title: On the Effects of Information-Bit Puncturing in Short-Length 5G NR LDPC Codes
Reason: 5G NR LDPC 단블록 정보비트 puncturing 영향 분석, 유한길이 코드설계(E) 이식 여지 — 애매하나 살림
Authors: Jungim Kim, Yongseouk Choi, Heesang Chung, Youngjo Ko
Abstract: In 5G NR systems, the three primary usage scenarios are enhanced Mobile Broadband (eMBB), Ultra-Reliable Low-Latency Communications (URLLC), and massive Machine Type Communications (mMTC). For data channels, LDPC codes were adopted as the standard channel coding scheme, initially optimized to meet the high-throughput requirements of eMBB services. As part of the LDPC coding process, a fixed puncturing strategy is applied to the information bits, which has been shown to improve system performance in eMBB scenarios. However, in URLLC and mMTC services, where transmissions are typically limited to short block lengths, the effect of information-bit puncturing may differ significantly. This paper investigates the impact of such puncturing on system performance under these short-block conditions.

Title: An FPGA Accelerator for CCSDS 142 O3K-LDPC for Non-Coherent Optical Communications Coding and Synchronization
Reason: CCSDS O3K-LDPC FPGA 가속기 — 신규 스케줄링/로드밸런싱 및 파이프라인 HW 아키텍처(D) 이식 가능
Authors: Dimitris Theodoropoulos, Christos Apostolidis, Nektarios Kranitis, Antonis Paschalis
Abstract: Nowadays, Free Space Optical Communications (FSOCs) such as Direct-to-Earth links have experienced explosive growth as they enable faster and more secure communications with lower power consumption compared to traditional radio frequency links. In this paper, we present the design, hardware implementation, and validation of a novel FPGA accelerator that implements O3K-LDPC telemetry signaling, as specified in the Consultative Committee for Space Data Systems (CCSDS) 142.0-P-1.1 standard for Non-Coherent Optical Communications Coding and Synchronization, in the form of a technology-agnostic IP core. The introduced CCSDS O3K-LDPC IP core accelerates the entire O3K-LDPC transmitter data-processing chain and introduces novel scheduling and load-balancing of LDPC operations. A pipelined, frame-to-frame data processing architecture with optimized FIFO-based shift-update scheduling enables continuous processing and significant resource/power efficiency. Targeting Xilinx COTS Zynq-7000 for New-Space and Kintex UltraScale space-grade FPGAs, it sustains a >10 Gb/s channel data rate and offers high-speed AXI4-Stream interfaces for seamless on-board integration. The IP core underwent extensive VHDL-RTL simulation-based verification against a bit-accurate MATLAB golden model and precomputed CCSDS golden MD5 hashes using Siemens Questa and the VUnit Python framework, with a 100% VHDL code-coverage target, and was validated on hardware on a AMD KCU-105 hosting a KU040 FPGA for institutional missions and GOMspace NanoMind HP MK3 hosting Zynq-7030 for New-Space. Flight heritage is expected by early 2026 on the ERMIS CubeSat under ESA AO/1-11498/22/UK/ND (Greek CubeSats In-Orbit Validation Projects).
